REPLY MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO LIFT STAY (TRANSACTION ID # 66034469) FILED BY PLAINTIFF GROVE, DANIEL ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED AND DERIVATIVELY ON BEHALF OF JUUL LABS, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DANIEL GROVE'S MOTION TO LIFT THE STAY IS GRANTED. THE COURT STAYED THIS ACTION TO ALLOW THE PARTIES' DELAWARE LITIGATION TO PROCEED AND THAT LITIGATION IS NOW OVER. DEFENDANT IMPLICITLY CONCEDES THAT LIFTING THE STAY IS APPROPRIATE AS IT SEEKS AFFIRMATIVE RELIEF BY WAY OF DISMISSAL IN ITS OPPOSITION. THE COURT DOES NOT ADDRESS THE PARTIES' ARGUMENTS REGARDING THE APPLICABILITY OF LABOR CODE SECTION 925, COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL, FULL FAITH AND CREDIT, ETC. AS THIS MOTION PRESENTS THE SINGLE LIMITED ISSUE WHETHER THE COURT SHOULD LIFT THE STAY. AS STATED, THERE IS GOOD CAUSE TO LIFT THE STAY. THE COURT CANNOT OPINE REGARDING AS YET UNFILED MOTIONS AND OTHER ISSUES NOT PRESENTLY BEFORE IT. THE PARTIES ARE INSTRUCTED TO MEET AND CONFER AND AGREE UPON A MUTUALLY CONVENIENT HEARING DATE FOR ANY SUBSEQUENT MOTIONS RELATED TO THE ISSUES DISCUSSED IN THEIR BRIEFS, E.G., WHETHER THE ACTION SHOULD BE DISMISSED OR WHETHER PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO RELIEF UNDER LABOR CODE SEC. 925. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JUUL LABS, INC.'S MOTION TO SEAL. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. GRANT. NO OPPOSITION FILED, AND THE MOTION COMPLIES WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULES 2.550 AND 2.551. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE PORTIONS OF THE RECORDS TO BE SEALED; THE OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THOSE RECORDS; A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE RECORD IS NOT SEALED; THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED; AND NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR# 6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Void Provisions In Contract Pursuant To Cal. Labor Code 925
Off Calendar Dec-17-2020 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-17-2020 Off Calendar

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift The Stay
Off Calendar Oct-26-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF DANIEL GROVE'S Motion To Lift The Stay is granted. The court stayed this action to allow the parties' Delaware litigation to proceed and that litigation is now over. Defendant implicitly concedes that lifting the stay is appropriate as it seeks affirmative relief by way of dismissal in its opposition. The Court does not address the parties' arguments regarding the applicability of Labor Code section 925, collateral estoppel, Full Faith and Credit, etc. as this motion presents the single limited issue whether the Court should lift the stay. As stated, there is good cause to lift the stay. The Court cannot opine regarding as yet unfiled motions and other issues not presently before it. The parties are instructed to meet and confer and agree upon a mutually convenient hearing date for any subsequent motions related to the issues discussed in their briefs, e.g., whether the action should be dismissed or whether plaintiff is entitled to relief under Labor Code sec. 925. Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: not reported.

Francisc Bottini Esq. 858-914-2001 of Bottini & Bottini Inc. for Plaintiff Daniel Grove.
Bruce Ericson, Esq. 415-983-1560; Lee Brand, Esq. 415-983-1116 of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP for Defendant Juul Labs.

DEFENDANT JUUL LABS, INC.'s Motion To Seal.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Grant. No opposition filed, and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay For Forum Non Conveniens And Supporting Memorandum
Off Calendar Jul-01-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (all through Court Call):
Francisc Bottini Esq. 858-914-2001 of Bottini & Bottini Inc. for Plaintiff Daniel Grove.
Bruce Ericson, Esq. 415-983-1560; Lee Brand, Esq. 415-983-1116 of Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP for Defendant Juul Labs.

RULING - A R G U E D. PLainttiff argues.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT JUUL LABS, INC.'s Motion To Stay For Forum Non Conveniens.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to stay proceedings on the basis of forum non conveniens is granted on the basis of the mandatory forum selection clauses in the parties' written agreements.
"[I]f there is a mandatory forum selection clause, the test is simply whether application of the clause is unfair or unreasonable, and the clause is usually given effect." (Berg v. MTC Electronics Technologies (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 349, 358.) The Exercise Agreement contains a clause stating there is "exclusive jurisdiction of any state or federal court sitting in the State of Delaware for the purpose of enforcing any provision of this Agreement?." (Laconsay Decl., Ex. 2 6.) The Investors' Rights Agreement contains a closely similar provision. (Id., Ex. 3 5.11(d).) Plaintiff's argument that this action is not subject to the clause because it is not based on asserted rights in the Exercise Agreement is unpersuasive. "Whether the clause applies to the facts of this case . . . presents a preliminary question of contract interpretation." (Bushansky v. Soon-Shiong (2018) 23 Cal.App.5th 1000, 1005-1006, internal citations omitted.) "The language of a contract is to govern its interpretation, if the language is clear and explicit, and does not involve an absurdity." (Civ. Code 1638.) The plain language here is clear and explicit. The clause applies "for the purpose of enforcing any provision of this Agreement," and the Exercise Agreement contains a provision that defendant contends unconditionally and irrevocably waives Plaintiff's rights to inspect Juul's books and records. (Laconsay Decl., Ex. 2 1.) Thus, the forum selection clause must be given effect.
"[W]here a 'mandatory' forum selection clause is involved, the party opposing enforcement of the clause bears the burden of proof" of proving that application would be unfair or unreasonable." (Bushansky, 23 Cal.App.5th at 1006.) Plaintiff does not show that application of the clauses would be unfair or unreasonable, particularly given that defendant is a Delaware corporation, the agreements are governed by Delaware law, the parties are already engaged in litigation in Delaware concerning the same issues, and neither has contested jurisdiction in Delaware. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
